item.title: Pakistan envoy meets PM’s defence adviser
item.description: Pakistan high commissioner to Bangladesh Imran Haider on Sunday paid a courtesy call on prime minister’s defence adviser retired Brigadier General AKM Shamsul Islam at the armed forces division in Dhaka Cantonment......City& Local Guides

item.title: Harassment in football screening at DU sparks moral policing row
item.description: Several dozen female students of Dhaka University gathered at the Dr Muhammad Shahidullah Hall playground on Sunday morning to watch the Argentina vs Jordan World Cup match in protest of the alleged harassment of six former students of the university, including a female student, on Saturday......

item.title: Opposition focuses only on reforms for share of power: Muhit
item.description: State minister for health MA Muhit criticised the opposition, saying that it only talked about reforms because it would give it a share of power......

item.title: Brazil face Japan test
item.description: Brazil’s World Cup Round of 32 meeting with Japan on Monday ​comes with the chance for revenge for Carlo Ancelotti’s squad and a test of how far he has taken ‌his team since a Tokyo collapse early in his tenure......

item.title: Vitamin A campaign launched after 15 months
item.description: The government on Sunday launched the National Vitamin A Plus Campaign with a target of administering Vitamin A capsules to 24 million children aged between six and 59 months across Bangladesh in a single day......ExecutiveBranch

item.title: Detention continues even after bail
item.description: The continued detention of accused persons even after they secure bail from the Supreme Court or release orders from subordinate courts has triggered fresh concerns among senior lawyers and rights defenders over personal liberty, due process, and effective independence of the judiciary......

item.title: Protests erupt over outages as power shortage hits 3,500MW
item.description: Aggrieved locals on Sunday took to the streets, blocking roads, including the Dhaka-Tangail highway, and forming human chains in protest against frequent power cuts amid a heatwave sweeping parts of Bangladesh......

item.title: Legalising undisclosed money may be dropped
item.description: The government is likely to drop the proposal for voluntary declaration and legalisation of undisclosed income from previous transactions involving buildings and flats from the proposed Finance Bill 2026, finance ministry officials said on Sunday......

item.title: Defaulted farm loans hit record Tk 20,130cr
item.description: Defaulted loans in Bangladesh’s agricultural sector surged to Tk 20,130.18 crore by the end of May 2026, accounting for 31.53 per cent of the sector’s total outstanding loans, highlighting mounting repayment risks despite strong credit expansion......GeographicReference

item.title: 12 Chinese cos propose $9.2b investment in Bangladesh
item.description: Twelve Chinese companies have proposed investments worth $9.21 billion in Bangladesh across energy, infrastructure, logistics, manufacturing and education sectors following meetings with prime minister Tarique Rahman during his recent visit to China......

item.title: Experts call for regulatory reforms for MSME growth
item.description: Experts on Sunday called for simplifyingbusinessregulations, introducing a one-stop digital licensing system, and improving access to business support services to spur growth in the country’s micro, small, and medium enterprises sector......

item.title: Govt aims to boost livestock exports: Tuku
item.description: State minister for fisheries and livestock Sultan Salauddin Tuku on Sunday said that the government had set a target to export high-quality livestock products to the international market after meeting domestic demands......

item.title: Govt must break MSME informality barrier
item.description: THE growth of micro, small and medium enterprises, considered the backbone of emerging economies, has for long been caught in the web of formalisation, or the lack of it, which prevents them from accessing the finance that they need to increase their productivity. The 2024 Bureau of Statistics data show that there are 11.7 million MSME entities, but only 3.28 million......

item.title: Law enforcement in chars needs to be stepped up
item.description: LAW and order in char lands of the River Padma spanning Rajshahi, Natore, Pabna and Kushtia has alarmingly declined, exposing the government’s inability at protecting some most remote yet vulnerable communities. At least seven people were killed in nine months in the chars while others have disappeared or became injured in attacks linked to disputes over sand......

item.title: Why Dhaka can’t afford binary foreign policy
item.description: THE world is increasingly becoming impatient with ambiguity. No matter which regions or what issues are involved, countries find themselves under increasing pressure to take sides. Competition between the United States and China is growing. Economics and security become ever more intertwined. Technology becomes geopolitics. Trade deals acquire strategic......

item.title: With no FeLV vaccine, what if you’re next?
item.description: JUST days ago, one of my cats was diagnosed with Feline Leukaemia Virus (FeLV), a retrovirus I knew little about until she suddenly developed fever, lethargy and loss of appetite. Although she was taken to a veterinary clinic as soon as she became ill, she was not tested until I specifically requested it, nor was she given proper supportive treatment her condition......

item.title: Forgotten micro-entrepreneurs
item.description: THE most remarkable entrepreneurs in Bangladesh may be the ones we rarely recognise as entrepreneurs. They are the widow who turns a home kitchen into a small catering business after losing her family’s primary breadwinner, the retired teacher who channels decades of experience into a neighbourhood coaching centre to supplement an inadequate pension, and......

item.title: Govt to cut cos registration time to 14 days, minister tells JS
item.description: The government was planning to reduce the time required to establish a new company and reach the stage of opening letters of credit for importing machinery to just 14 days from an average of 355 days, commerce minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir told Jatiya Sangsad on Sunday......News

item.title: Four more die of suspected measles
item.description: Four more patients have died from suspected measles across Bangladesh in the past 24 hours till Sunday morning, according to the Directorate General of Health Services......

item.title: Govt backs pvt sector to drive 10,000MW solar power by 2030
item.description: Power and energy minister Iqbal Hasan Mahmood on Sunday said the government was prioritising private investment to achieve its target of generating 10,000 megawatts of solar electricity by 2030......

item.title: PM orders SPARRSO modernisation during surprise visit
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Sunday directed the authorities concerned to develop the Bangladesh Space Research and Remote Sensing Organisation as a modern, future-ready, self-reliant space research institution capable of making effective contributions to national security and development......

item.title: No scope for HSC question leak rumours: minister
item.description: Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Haque Milon on Sunday said the government would not allow anyone to create confusion by spreading rumours of question paper leaks ahead of the Higher Secondary Certificate and equivalent examinations......ExecutiveBranch

item.title: Coexistence of nationalists, Islamists key to resolving nat’l crisis: seminar
item.description: Speakers at a seminar in Dhaka on Sunday said that the peaceful coexistence between Bengali nationalists and Islamists could help resolve the country’s ongoing crisis, despite the current political landscape being marked by a sharp binary divide......

item.title: Public projects must ensure inclusivity: Saki
item.description: State minister for planning Zonayed Abdur Rahim Saki on Sunday said that public projects must ensure accessibility, inclusivity and space for ordinary people, including persons with disabilities......

item.title: UGC signs memo with Tianjin University in China
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh on Sunday signed a memorandum of understanding with the Tianjin University in China to strengthen cooperation in higher education, research, teacher development, scholarship programmes, academic exchanges and postgraduate education between Bangladesh and China......

item.title: Couple jailed for 7 years for torturing domestic help
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday sentenced a couple – Mostakin Sharif and his wife Jannatul Naima – to seven years’ imprisonment in a case filed over the torture of a child domestic worker at their South Banasree house in 2018......GeographicReference

item.title: Schoolgirl among four killed in road accidents
item.description: At least four people, including a schoolgirl and a teacher, were killed and one was injured in road accidents in Cox’s Bazar, Tangail and Jashore districts on Sunday......

item.title: Europe swelters as heatwave moves east
item.description: Europe’s deadly heatwave pushed east Sunday with hundreds of millions still sweltering across the continent despite fleeting relief from overnight storms, notably in France and Belgium......

item.title: Fakhrul urges Jamaat to apologise for 1971 role
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party secretary general and local government minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Sunday called on Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami to apologise to the nation for its role during the 1971 War of Independence, saying such an apology would make the party’s political path easier......

item.title: Ex-AL MP Nur Mohammad on remand
item.description: A metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Sunday allowed the police to take former Awami League lawmaker for the Jamalpur-1 constituency, Nur Mohammad, in custody for three days for interrogation in a case filed with Tejgaon industrial police station under the Anti-Terrorism Act......Politics

item.title: Nahid calls budget ‘a document of deception’
item.description: National Citizen Party lawmaker and opposition chief whip Nahid Islam slammed the proposed national budget, asking whether it would be wrong to call it a ‘document of deception’......

item.title: Child labour elimination target missed
item.description: Over 10 lakh children in Bangladesh continue to work in dangerous conditions with minimal government oversight, exposing the nation’s future to abuse, torture, and severe physical harms......

item.title: PM directs nationalisation of Bandarban Tindu school
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman has asked the education ministry for nationalising the Tindu Junior Secondary School, located in the remote Tindu area of Thanchi upazila in Bandarban......
